Member.title: Member
Member.description: Represents a member of the Transcend Global Network Hub.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Member entity.

tgnMemberId.description: Unique TGN Member ID assigned during onboarding.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the member's profile image.

MentorCertification.title: MentorCertification
MentorCertification.description: Represents the certification status of a mentor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MentorCertification entity.

programCompletion.description: Indicates if the mentor has completed the certification program.

isVerifiedAssociate.description: Indicates if the mentor is a verified associate.

verificationDate.description: The date the mentor was verified.

MenteeVerification.title: MenteeVerification
MenteeVerification.description: Represents the verification status of a mentee.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenteeVerification entity.

isVerified.description: Indicates if the mentee is verified.

verificationDate.description: The date the mentee was verified.

AffiliateEarning.title: AffiliateEarning
AffiliateEarning.description: Represents affiliate earnings for a member.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AffiliateEarning entity.

earningDate.description: The date the earning was made.

sourceType.description: The source of the earning (course, program, event, book).

sourceId.description: Reference to the source entity (e.g., Course.id).

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product sold in the e-commerce marketplace.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

approvalStatus.description: The approval status of the product (pending, approved, rejected).

packageBenefits.description: Configurable package benefits for the product.

Match.title: Match
Match.description: Represents a match between two members.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Match entity.

memberId1.description: Reference to the first Member in the match. (Relationship: Member 1:N Match)

memberId2.description: Reference to the second Member in the match. (Relationship: Member 1:N Match)

matchType.description: The type of match (mentor, program, sector, community, event).

matchId.description: Reference to the matched entity (e.g., Mentor.id, Program.id).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

startTime.description: The start time of the event.

endTime.description: The end time of the event.

definition.description: Stores member profiles. Allows listing of members (QAP).

params.name: memberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the member.

definition.description: Stores mentor certifications for each user. Path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: mentorCertificationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the mentor certification.

definition.description: Stores mentee verifications for each user. Path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: menteeVerificationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the mentee verification.

definition.description: Stores affiliate earnings for each user. Path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: affiliateEarningId
params.description: The unique identifier of the affiliate earning.

definition.description: Stores product information. The `sellerId` field is used for access control.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

params.name: matchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the match.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier of the event.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to support the Transcend Global Network Hub application, focusing on secure and scalable data access based on the core design principles outlined. The design emphasizes Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), QAPs (Rules are not Filters) and Invariants.

**Authorization Independence:**

*   The design avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies (`get()`). Access control relies primarily on the user's `uid` and roles stored directly within the documents or in dedicated role collections, enabling atomic operations.

**Structural Segregation:**

*   Different data types with different access requirements are stored in separate collections. Private user data is kept under `/users/{userId}`, while more public or collaborative data (e.g., events, products) reside in top-level collections with membership maps for access control.

**Access Modeling:**

*   **Private Data:** User-specific data (e.g., mentee verification, mentor certification, affiliate earnings) is stored under `/users/{userId}` for path-based ownership.
*   **Collaborative Data (Products):** Products use the `products` collection, with the `sellerId` field for identifying the seller. Although seemingly violating authorization independence, this is acceptable as ownership is directly on the document. Read access can be public, but writes must be restricted to the seller.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** The design doesn't explicitly use global roles stored in collections like `/roles_admin/{uid}` in this iteration, but this can be added if needed for administrative functions. The current structure relies on user-specific data and collaborative data.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   The structure supports secure list operations by segregating data with different access requirements into different collections. For example, only authenticated users can list members via `/members` (requiring proper security rules for filtering based on some public attribute), while private member details are protected under `/users/{userId}`.

*Denormalization Strategy:*
Access control is made independent by denormalizing access related data. In the /products collection, the `sellerId` property holds the uid of the document's owner. This allows security rules to make access decisions based solely on the contents of the document, without needing to perform costly and brittle `get()` calls to parent or related documents.
